mnemonics.title: Environment Properties Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Use FADS-C: Fully/Partially observable, Deterministic/Stochastic, Static/Dynamic, Discrete/Continuous, Single/Multi-agent, Benign/Adversarial - This helps remember all key environment properties in order.

mnemonics.title: PEAS Recall Trick
mnemonics.content: Think of an agent as a worker: Performance = what they're paid for, Environment = where they work, Actuators = their tools/hands, Sensors = their eyes/ears. Just like evaluating a real worker's performance!

mnemonics.title: Observable vs Partially Observable
mnemonics.content: FULLY = all visible (like chess board). PARTIALLY = hidden cards (like poker). Remember: 'FULLY see everything, PARTIALy hide some' - partial rhymes with partial information.

acronyms.usage: Framework for defining task environments by specifying what defines success, where the agent operates, what it can do, and what it perceives.

acronyms.expansion: Fully/Partially observable, Deterministic/Stochastic, Static/Dynamic, Discrete/Continuous, Single/Multi-agent, Benign/Adversarial
acronyms.usage: Mnemonic to remember all key properties used to classify environments in AI.
A mathematical framework for decision-making in environments where the agent cannot fully observe the state. Used for partially observable, stochastic environments.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine looking at a chess board (fully observable - everything visible) versus looking at playing cards face-down on a table (partially observable - you don't know their values).
Think of a predictable row of dominoes falling (deterministic - same result every time) versus rolling dice (stochastic - random outcome each time).
A still photograph (static - nothing changes until you act on it) versus a busy intersection with moving cars (dynamic - changes continuously regardless of your actions).

analogies.analogy: Think of the environment as a theater stage where the agent is the actor. The stage (environment) has properties like lighting (observable), whether the set changes during the play (static/dynamic), and whether there are other actors (single/multi-agent). The agent must understand these properties to perform well.

An AI agent exploring an environment is like a explorer navigating terrain. Some terrain is fully visible (like a cleared map), some is foggy (partially observable). Some terrain changes unpredictably (dynamic), while some stays constant (static). The explorer must adapt their strategy based on the terrain type.

PEAS is like a job description: Performance (goals), Environment (workplace), Actuators (tools), Sensors (information sources). Just as a employee needs to understand their job description, an AI agent needs PEAS to understand its task.
